name: Tunnel of Fudge Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and flour a 12-cup fluted tube pan or a 10-inch tube pan.
3. In a large bowl, beat the sugar and margarine until light and fluffy.
4. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Gradually add 2 cups powdered sugar; blend well.
6. Lightly spoon flour into a measuring cup; level off. Stir in the flour and remaining cake ingredients with a hand mixer until well blended.
7. Spoon the batter into the greased and floured pan and spread evenly.
8. Bake for 58 to 62 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
9. Cool the cake upright in the pan on a wire rack for 1 hour.
10. Invert the cake onto a serving plate and allow it to cool completely.
11. In a small bowl, blend 3/4 cup powdered sugar, 1/4 cup cocoa powder, and enough milk for the desired drizzling consistency.
12. Spoon the glaze over the cooled cake, allowing some to run down the sides.
TIPS: Nuts are essential for the success of this recipe. Since the cake has a soft tunnel of fudge, an ordinary doneness test cannot be used. Accurate oven temperature and baking time are essential. Calories: 560. Source: Pillsbury Bake Off Cookbook
